name: adb
title: adb
description:
Android Debug Bridge
A versatile command line tool that lets you communicate with an emulator
instance or connected Android-powered device.
.
This package depends on "android-udev-rules" which contains
the udev rules for Android devices. Without this package, adb and fastboot need
to be running with root permission.

commands.name: adb
commands.description: CLI Client for ADB (Android Debug Bridge) Server

arguments.description: Listen on all network interfaces, not just localhost.
arguments.raw: -a  Listen on all network interfaces, not just localhost.

arguments.description: Override platform's default ABI.
arguments.raw: --abi  Override platform's default ABI.

arguments.description: Use USB device (error if multiple devices connected).
arguments.raw: -d  Use USB device (error if multiple devices connected).

arguments.description: deployment agent when local version is newer and using fast de-
arguments.raw: -date-check-agent  deployment agent when local version is newer and using fast de-

arguments.description: Use TCP/IP device (error if multiple TCP/IP devices available).
arguments.raw: -e  Use TCP/IP device (error if multiple TCP/IP devices available).

arguments.description: Exit if stdout is closed.
arguments.raw: --exit-on-write-error  Exit if stdout is closed.

arguments.description: Use fast deploy.
arguments.raw: --fastdeploy  Use fast deploy.

arguments.description: Force update of deployment agent when using fast deploy.
arguments.raw: -force-agent  Force update of deployment agent when using fast deploy.

arguments.description: Grant all runtime permissions.
arguments.raw: -g  Grant all runtime permissions.

arguments.description: Cause the app to be installed as an ephemeral install app.
arguments.raw: --instant  Cause the app to be installed as an ephemeral install app.

arguments.description: Keep the data and cache directories.
arguments.raw: -k  Keep the data and cache directories.

arguments.description: Listen on given socket for adb server [default=tcp:localhost:5037].
arguments.raw: -L  Listen on given socket for adb server [default=tcp:localhost:5037].

arguments.description: Use long output.
arguments.raw: -l  Use long output.

arguments.description: List all forward socket connections.
arguments.raw: --list  List all forward socket connections.

arguments.description: agent files from local source build (instead of SDK location). See
arguments.raw: --local-agent  agent files from local source build (instead of SDK location). See

arguments.description: Dry run, push files to device without storing to the filesystem.
arguments.raw: -n  Dry run, push files to device without storing to the filesystem.

arguments.description: Prevent use of fast deploy.
arguments.raw: -no-fastdeploy  Prevent use of fast deploy.

arguments.description: Always push APK to device and invoke Package Manager as separate steps.
arguments.raw: --no-streaming  Always push APK to device and invoke Package Manager as separate steps.

arguments.description: Server will only connect to one USB device, specified by a SERIAL
arguments.raw: --one-device  Server will only connect to one USB device, specified by a SERIAL

arguments.description: Smart socket PORT of adb server [default=5037].
arguments.raw: -P  Smart socket PORT of adb server [default=5037].

arguments.description: Partial application install (install-multiple only).
arguments.raw: -p  Partial application install (install-multiple only).

arguments.description: Automatically reboot the device.
arguments.raw: -R  Automatically reboot the device.

arguments.description: Remove specific forward socket connection.
arguments.raw: --remove  Remove specific forward socket connection.

arguments.description: Remove all forward socket connections.
arguments.raw: --remove-all  Remove all forward socket connections.

arguments.description: Use device with given SERIAL (overrides $ANDROID_SERIAL).
arguments.raw: -s  Use device with given SERIAL (overrides $ANDROID_SERIAL).

arguments.description: Force streaming APK directly into Package Manager.
arguments.raw: --streaming  Force streaming APK directly into Package Manager.

arguments.description: Only push files that are newer on the host than the device.
arguments.raw: --sync  Only push files that are newer on the host than the device.

arguments.description: Use device with given transport ID.
arguments.raw: -t  Use device with given transport ID.

arguments.description: force pty allocation).
arguments.raw: -tt  force pty allocation).

arguments.description: Update deployment agent when local version has different version
arguments.raw: --version-check-agent  Update deployment agent when local version has different version

arguments.description: Disable remote exit codes and stdout/stderr separation.
arguments.raw: -x  Disable remote exit codes and stdout/stderr separation.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use adb because Android Debug Bridge.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use adb as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `adb [GLOBAL_OPTIONS] command [COMMAND_OPTIONS]` as documented upstream for adb.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair adb with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `adb --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: adb - CLI Client for ADB (Android Debug Bridge) Server
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Android Debug Bridge.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: adb - CLI Client for ADB (Android Debug Bridge) Server.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install android-platform-tools (34.0.5-13)
Install with: sudo apt install android-platform-tools (34.0.5-13)
